purpose: Large-format one-click git operations bar, optimized for non-coders. Renders a slim button row (with current branch chip) that opens a centered modal grid organized into four categories: Common (Save My Work, Update from Remote, Share My Changes), Remote (Fetch, Sync), Branch & Stash (Move to New Branch, Stash, Pop Stash), Undo / Reset (Undo Last Commit, Discard All Changes). Each action card has an icon, title, description, variant (primary/success/warning/danger), and disabled-with-reason. Selecting an action opens a confirmation dialog from SafetyDialogs (ConfirmDialog or DoubleConfirmDialog with type-DISCARD challenge for hard reset). Implements composite operations: 'Save My Work' = stageAll → commit (with auto-generated 'Update X, Y, and N other files' message); 'Move to New Branch' = stash → createBranch → stashPop with rollback; 'Sync' = pull → push; 'Undo Last Commit' = soft reset to HEAD~1; 'Discard All' = hard reset to HEAD with double confirmation requiring typed 'DISCARD'.
gotchas: generateCommitMessage produces semi-natural-language summaries: 'Update foo.js', 'Update foo.js and bar.js', 'Update foo.js, bar.js, and N other files' — these are NOT conventional commit format. If the project enforces conventional commits, this auto-message will fail commit hooks.

Move to New Branch is a 3-step composite: stash → createBranch → stashPop. If createBranch fails, it attempts gitStashPop to restore changes (best-effort, .catch(() => {})). If stashPop fails after createBranch succeeds, the user is told their changes are still in the stash list.
'Discard All Changes' uses DoubleConfirmDialog with confirmText='DISCARD' — user must type the word exactly to enable the final confirm. Affected file count is interpolated into the second message.
All disabled-state reasons are surfaced via the `title` attribute on the card, only when disabled — non-disabled cards have no tooltip.
Buttons that depend on hasRemotes (pull/push/fetch/sync) are disabled with reason 'No remote configured' — but RemotePanel may still expose Push, leading to slight redundancy.
Refresh after every action calls refreshGitData (status+branches+currentBranch+log) — a heavy refresh; metrics may suffer on huge repos.

The hard-reset 'Discard All Changes' destroys untracked files via gitReset hard — but the IPC handler may or may not also include `git clean -fd`. Verify the IPC implementation matches the user's expectation.
patterns: Single activeDialog state ('save'|'pull'|'push'|'fetch'|'sync'|'moveBranch'|'stash'|'stashPop'|'undoCommit'|'discardAll') controls which ConfirmDialog renders — mutually exclusive.
Dangerlevel mapping: safe (pull/push/fetch/sync/save/stash/stashPop), warning (undoCommit, moveBranch via inline dialog), danger (discardAll with type-to-confirm).
Modal closes on Escape via window keydown listener attached only when modalOpen.
showSuccess sets a transient success message that auto-clears after 3 seconds — distinct from per-dialog error which lives in `error` state.
conventions: Each handle*Confirm function follows the pattern: setLoading(true) → setError(null) → try/catch IPC call → refreshGitData → setActiveDialog(null) → showSuccess(...).

Inline SVG icons (SaveIcon, MoveBranchIcon, UndoIcon, DiscardIcon, PullIcon, PushIcon, StashIcon, StashPopIcon, FetchIcon, SyncIcon, plus small ChevronIcon, BranchSmallIcon, CloseIcon, SuccessCheckIcon, ErrorXIcon) — large icons for cards, small icons for chips/buttons.
Move to New Branch uses an inline custom dialog (NOT ConfirmDialog) because it needs a text input.

depends_on: electron/preload.js (gitStatus, gitStageAll, gitCommit, gitStash, gitStashPop, gitCreateBranch, gitReset, gitPull, gitPush, gitFetch, gitBranches, gitCurrentBranch, gitLog)
